name: nika-lang
description: See your workflow before it runs: the live DAG canvas for Nika, the workflow language for AI (.nika.yaml). Check-as-you-type diagnostics, run + time-travel replay, and a static audit before a token is spent (cost ceiling · secret-flow · permits). Local-first: Ollama, llama.cpp, vLLM, Mistral + more. For VS Code, Cursor, Windsurf, VSCodium.

untrustedWorkspaces.description: In Restricted Mode the workspace cannot override which binary the extension executes; static language features stay on.

virtualWorkspaces.description: Diagnostics, graph and runs need a real file system and the nika binary; syntax, snippets and expression intel work everywhere.

colors.description: The infer verb's canonical hue (nika.sh verb canon, design SSOT): tints infer task icons.
The exec verb's canonical hue (nika.sh verb canon, design SSOT): tints exec task icons.
The invoke verb's canonical hue (nika.sh verb canon, design SSOT): tints invoke task icons.
The agent verb's canonical hue (nika.sh verb canon, design SSOT): tints agent task icons.

commands.title: Run Workflow with Inputs (and a spend ceiling)
Preflight (cost · secrets · permits, before any token)
Quiet a Finding in This Workspace
Diff Two Runs on the DAG
Export Run to OpenTelemetry (Jaeger · Grafana · Langfuse)
Fork From Task (upstream rehydrates from the trace)
Run Report (provable · read from the trace)
Run Detail (the run at a glance)
Export History as a Document (shareable · diffable)
Run Report (provable · read from the trace)
Show Task in DAG (replay this run · center the task)
Capture Lint Baseline (accept current findings as the baseline)
Update the Golden (rewrites the pin from this run)
Apply Verb Colors (canonical hues, every theme)
Finish Setup (engine + MCP + LSP, one gesture)
Tree Actions (every action of the focused row)

nika.server.path.markdownDescription: Path to the nika binary. `nika` means: resolve from PATH. Point this at a dev build and every surface (check · graph · runs · LSP) speaks through that binary instead.

nika.server.autoDownload.markdownDescription: When no binary resolves from `#nika.server.path#`, offer a verified download from GitHub releases (HTTPS · SHA-256 checked · explicit consent first). Off: the status bar and welcome views keep the install affordance, nothing is fetched.

nika.server.extraArgs.markdownDescription: Extra arguments appended to the `nika lsp` launch. Wrong flags stop the server from starting: the status pill will say so.

nika.inlayHints.enabled.markdownDescription: Inline ghost annotations at line ends (timeout, model cost, binding labels). Off: the facts stay in hovers and the canvas only.

nika.codeLens.enabled.markdownDescription: Actionable lenses above tasks (Run · Validate · task count · last run status). Off: the same verbs stay reachable from the palette and the status menu.

nika.nudge.firstCleanCheck.markdownDescription: Once per workspace, when a workflow first checks clean, suggest the next step (run it · mock/echo needs no key). Off: no suggestion; the verdict still lands in the Problems panel.

enumDescriptions: One line per LSP request/response: enough to see what fired
Full message payloads: heavy, for deep protocol debugging
nika.trace.server.markdownDescription: Trace communication between VS Code and the Nika language server into the output channel. Anything above `off` grows the log fast: turn on to diagnose, back off after.

nika.intel.enabled.markdownDescription: Client-side template intelligence inside `${{ … }}` islands: completions, hover, go-to-definition for tasks/with/env/secrets/vars refs. Off: those islands go quiet (engine diagnostics keep working).

enumDescriptions: After each edit, debounced: findings track your typing (a check spawn per pause)
Only when the file is saved: quieter machine, staler squiggles between saves
Never: no squiggles, no quick fixes; `nika check` in a terminal stays the oracle
nika.diagnostics.runOn.markdownDescription: When to run `nika check --json` for editor diagnostics. Squiggles appear later (or never) as you move down the ladder; quick fixes ride the diagnostics, so `off` parks them too.

nika.diagnostics.workspace.markdownDescription: Lint CLOSED `*.nika.yaml` files across the workspace (`nika check --json`) so every workflow shows in the Problems panel. Capped at 300 files per sweep (logged when hit); open files stay with the live controller. Off: only open files report.

nika.diagnostics.severity.markdownDescription: Remap finding severity per code: exact (`NIKA-DAG-003`), glob family (`NIKA-SEC-*`), or client lints (`nika.literal-secret` · `nika.redundant-dep` · `nika.unused-schema`). Exact keys win over globs; `off` hides the squiggle (quick fixes stay available).

nika.secretsLint.enabled.markdownDescription: Warn on literal credentials pasted in workflow YAML (pure local scan) with a `${{ env.VAR }}` quick fix. Off: pasted keys sit unflagged until review.

nika.traces.keep.markdownDescription: Journal housekeeping: keep the newest N run journals per workflow directory (0 = unlimited). Above the cap, oldest journals are deleted after each run; paused journals and the active resume target always survive.

nika.traces.glob.markdownDescription: Where the Runs view (and the xray ghosts, see `#nika.editor.xray#`) look for flight-recorder traces. Narrow it to skip vendored trees; a glob that misses `.nika/traces` empties the Runs view.

nika.traces.live.markdownDescription: Live-overlay a growing trace onto the open DAG panel while an engine writes a run: a run launched in ANY terminal animates the graph. Off: the canvas repaints only when you replay (see `#nika.replay.speed#`).

nika.run.liveDag.markdownDescription: Run workflows by streaming `nika run --json` into the DAG panel live (statuses paint in real time). Off: runs open a terminal for raw engine output instead.

nika.replay.speed.markdownDescription: Trace replay time compression in the DAG webview (6 = 6× faster than recorded, engine default). 1 = real time; playback keeps its own floor and ceiling so every run stays watchable.

enumDescriptions: The Nika design language: warm engineered-black canvas, one blue accent, verb-hue tiles, Martian Mono (the brand skin, pinned dark)
Adaptive: derives every surface AND the accent from your editor theme (--vscode-* variables, focusBorder as accent)
Phosphor: true-black OLED register, phosphor-green ink, hairlines; verb chroma sleeps at rest and wakes only on LIVE tasks (explicit choice · auto never picks it)
Auto: the brand skin on dark themes, the adaptive skin on light themes
nika.dag.theme.description: Visual skin of the DAG panel. High-contrast themes and forced colors always win over every skin.

nika.dag.followActiveEditor.markdownDescription: The open DAG panel re-targets when you switch to another `.nika.yaml` (debounced). Off: the panel stays on the workflow it was opened for until you ask again.

nika.editor.xray.markdownDescription: Ghost values: inlay hints show what each `${{ tasks.x… }}` ref resolved to in the last matching recorded run. Needs a trace on disk (see `#nika.traces.glob#`): no run, no ghosts.

nika.editor.runHighlight.markdownDescription: Light the YAML spans of RUNNING tasks while a run or replay is at them: the source is the timeline. Off: the editor sits still during runs.

nika.dag.cursorSync.markdownDescription: Soft halo in the DAG on the task your editor caret is in (same workflow · panel visible). Off: caret moves leave the canvas untouched.

nika.decorations.verbDots.markdownDescription: Verb-tinted gutter dots on each task line: the nika.sh verb canon (infer blue · exec orange · invoke cyan · agent violet). Off: the gutter is the theme's alone.

nika.editor.runDecorations.markdownDescription: End-of-line run badges per task after a run (✓ duration · cost; failed in red): the run lives in the editor. Off: verdicts stay in the Runs view and the canvas.

nika.ai.toolsEnabled.markdownDescription: Register the Nika Language Model Tools (check · explain · graph · workspace on 0.104+ binaries) so in-editor AI agents validate workflows through the real oracle. Off: agents edit blind, no oracle access.

nika.search.aliases.markdownDescription: Your own shortcuts for the root search (`⌘K ⌘M`): map a two-or-three-letter alias to the command or row it launches, e.g. `{ "rw": "nika.runWorkflow", "gd": "nika.showDag" }`. Typing the exact alias ranks its row first, above every learned habit; the row teaches it back as `· rw`. Aliases never match partially, and one whose target the catalog lacks is skipped in silence.

nika.autoSetup.markdownDescription: On first activation with the binary present, wire MCP for this host automatically (`nika wire` is idempotent). Off: nothing is written until you run `Nika: Setup MCP + Agent Rules` yourself.

nika.explorerBadge.markdownDescription: Show the 🦋 badge on `*.nika.yaml` files in the Explorer. Icon themes own the file icon slot; the badge adds nika identity without replacing your theme. Off: files carry the theme icon alone.

languageModelTools.name: nika_check
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Statically validate a Nika workflow (.nika.yaml) BEFORE running it. Returns the full machine-readable pre-flight report: conformance violations, secret leaks/egresses, permits (capability) escapes with machine-applicable fixes, schema findings, unknown tools, cost ceiling. You MUST call this after EVERY create or edit of a .nika.yaml file, and fix every finding before declaring the task done: never report success while findings remain. Not for generic YAML or GitHub Actions files.

filePath.description: Absolute path to the .nika.yaml file (defaults to the active editor).

languageModelTools.name: nika_explain
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Explain a NIKA-XXXX error code: cause, category, and the fix form. Use when a check report or diagnostic carries a code you do not recognize.

code.description: The error code, e.g. NIKA-440 (bare 440 also accepted).

languageModelTools.name: nika_graph
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Project the canonical execution DAG of a Nika workflow (graph_format 2 JSON): topologically sorted task nodes with verb, model, when-gates, fan-out and static cost intervals, plus TYPED edges (value/terminal-observation/failure-observation carry a with: binding · control carries its after: predicate · recovery is on_error.recover's parking read). Requires the workflow to pass conformance first.

filePath.description: Absolute path to the .nika.yaml file (defaults to the active editor).

languageModelTools.name: nika_workspace
languageModelTools.modelDescription: The whole Nika workspace truth in ONE call (context_version 1 JSON): every *.nika.yaml audited (clean/strict verdicts, findings count, tasks, waves, per-verb breakdown, cost ceiling with FLOOR honesty, permits), the most recent recorded runs folded from the flight recorder (verdict, spend, unpriced calls), environment facts (wired clients, local providers, cloud key COUNTS, never values), and cross-file rollups. Arrays are capped and counted. Call this FIRST to orient before reading or editing workflows. Requires a nika binary with the workspace aggregate (`welcome --deep` on 0.104+, `context` on older dev builds); without one this tool is not registered.

command.description: The nika subcommand to run (check · run · inspect)

walkthroughs.title: Get started with Nika
walkthroughs.description: From zero to a proven, replayable AI workflow.

steps.title: Install the nika engine
steps.description:
The extension drives the `nika` binary (LSP · check · run · DAG).
[Finish Setup · one gesture](command:nika.finishSetup)
Verified download · MCP wired · LSP on. Prefer brew? `brew install supernovae-st/tap/nika`, then the same button detects it.

media.altText: One binary: the extension drives nika: check, run with local traces, language server, time-travel debug. Install via Finish Setup (verified download) or brew.

steps.title: Create your first workflow
steps.description:
Scaffold a `.nika.yaml` with the canonical envelope.
[New Workflow](command:nika.newWorkflow)

steps.title: Validate as you type
steps.description:
Diagnostics · completions · hover docs are live via the LSP, or run one explicit check.
[Validate Workflow](command:nika.checkWorkflow)

steps.title: Run it (no API key needed)
steps.description:
The starter uses `model: mock/echo` · deterministic · zero keys.
[Run Workflow](command:nika.runWorkflow)

steps.title: See the DAG
steps.description:
Visualize task dependencies · click a node to jump to its YAML.
[Show DAG](command:nika.showDag)

steps.title: Break it on purpose
steps.description:
The blank starter carries a commented `break_me:` task: uncomment it and run again. It fails BY DESIGN (offline · zero keys): the failed card carries its NIKA code where the prompt was: click it and the explain doc opens; the card's ⑂ forks from the failure; the Runs view keeps the autopsy. Created yours from an engine template? Make one more workflow and pick the blank starter.
[Run Workflow](command:nika.runWorkflow)

steps.title: Time-travel a recorded run (F5)
steps.description:
Set a breakpoint on any task line and press **F5**: the engine replays your latest recorded run under the debugger: step forward AND backward through settles, outputs in the Variables pane. Replay never re-executes.
[Debug the latest run](command:workbench.action.debug.start)

steps.title: Prove it ran (chain · reproduce · export)
steps.description:
Every run hash-chains its journal: the Runs view shows a shield when a journal fails verification, and the run report states its own integrity. Right-click a run → **Reproduce Run** classifies WHY two runs diverge (NONDETERMINISTIC names the flaky task). **Export to OpenTelemetry** ships the trace, chain head included, to Jaeger/Grafana/Langfuse.
[Open the Runs view](command:nikaRuns.focus)

steps.title: Make your AI an expert
steps.description:
`nika init` writes schema wiring, AGENTS.md, and Cursor rules; this command wires MCP so agents can check workflows and explain failures through the real oracle.
[Setup agents](command:nika.setupMcp)

steps.title: Keep it findable
steps.description:
Nika is independent open source: a star is how the next person finds it.
[Star on GitHub](https://github.com/supernovae-st/nika)

viewsWelcome.contents:
Nika turns repeatable AI work into files: one `.nika.yaml`, four verbs, audited before it runs.
The engine is not on this machine yet: every surface below waits on it.
[Finish setup · install engine + wire everything](command:nika.finishSetup)
Verified download (SHA256SUMS) · MCP · LSP: one gesture, everything lights up.
Prefer your own install? [Homebrew tap](https://github.com/supernovae-st/homebrew-tap) · [build from source](https://github.com/supernovae-st/nika#installation): both sovereign, no account.

This repo is not equipped for Nika yet.
[Init this project · scaffold + wire everything](command:nika.initProject)
Agent rules · MCP · schema wiring: one gesture, skip-if-exists.
[New session · wizard · describe · templates](command:nika.newSession)
[▶ Run the 10-second proof](command:nika.runProof)

No folder is open: workflows are files, and files need a home.
[Open Folder](command:workbench.action.files.openFolder)
No folder handy? The embedded examples read without one: [browse examples](command:nika.browseExamples).

No workflows here yet: this folder carries no `.nika.yaml` file.
[▶ Try the demo — offline, zero keys](command:nika.tryDemo)
A four-wave sandbox on mock/echo: press ▶ to watch it light up. Or [create your own](command:nika.newWorkflow): the wizard walks name · starter · model, offline with mock/echo.

Nika turns repeatable AI work into files: one `.nika.yaml`, four verbs, audited before it runs.
[New session · wizard · describe · templates](command:nika.newSession)
[Init project · scaffold + wire everything](command:nika.initProject)
[＋ New workflow](command:nika.newWorkflow)
[Describe → generate](command:nika.generateWorkflow)
[⧈ Browse embedded examples](command:nika.browseExamples)
[⊡ Open the canvas](command:nika.showDag)
Everything else lives under ⇧⌘P → “Nika”, or [open the menu](command:nika.showMenu).

No recorded runs yet: this view is the flight recorder: every run writes a tamper-evident, hash-chained trace here.
[▶ Run a workflow](command:nika.runWorkflow)
Mock needs no key: run with `mock/echo` for an offline, zero-spend rehearsal.
[⧈ Try an embedded example](command:nika.browseExamples)

The station lights up with the engine: install it and every row below becomes live machine truth.
[Finish setup · install engine + wire everything](command:nika.finishSetup)

No recorded runs match this workflow yet: run it once and the grid fills itself.
[▶ Run Workflow](command:nika.runWorkflow)
Mock needs no key: `mock/echo` rehearses offline, and every run still writes its trace.

workflow.description: The .nika.yaml the run executed (stack frames point here).

replay.description: A recorded run journal (.nika/traces/*.ndjson) · replay never re-executes.

configurationSnippets.label: Nika: Replay a recorded run
configurationSnippets.description: Time-travel debug over a .nika/traces journal (stepBack included).

scripts.vscode:prepublish: npm run clean && npm run compile
scripts.typecheck: tsc -p ./ && tsc --noEmit --strict --target es2020 --module es2022 --moduleResolution bundler --lib es2022,dom,dom.iterable src/webview/dag.ts src/webview/elkWorker.ts
scripts.test: vitest run && node scripts/parity.mjs && node scripts/tokens-parity.mjs && node scripts/voice-gate.mjs && node scripts/glyph-registry.mjs && node scripts/walkthrough-media.mjs && npm run lint
scripts.test:integration: npm run compile && npm run compile:integration && node out-integration/runTests.js && node out-integration/runFirstContact.js
scripts.test:e2e:first-contact: npm run compile && npm run compile:integration && node out-integration/runFirstContact.js
